text: The item Eviolite only works if the Pokemon is not fully evolved. Since Scyther and Scizor share the exact same Base Stat Total of 500, giving an Eviolite to Scyther actually gives it higher raw defensive stats than its evolution.
label: analysis

text: Toxic inflicts badly poisoned status, where the damage increases every turn. It starts at 1/16 of max HP and adds another 1/16 each subsequent turn, making it statistically impossible for any Pokemon without a healing move or status cure to survive past 6 turns.
label: analysis

text: Rapid Spin is a 50 base power move that removes entry hazards from your side of the field. Unlike Defog, it does not remove hazards you have set on the opponent's side, making it the mathematically optimal hazard removal tool for offensive teams.
label: analysis

text: The move Foul Play calculates its damage using the target's physical Attack stat rather than the user's. This allows defensively invested Pokemon like Umbreon to deal massive damage against physical sweepers like Garchomp without investing any EVs into their own Attack.
label: analysis

text: Weather abilities like Sand Stream boost the Special Defense of all Rock-type Pokemon by 50%. This is why Tyranitar is so difficult to KO with special attacks, as its effective base Special Defense jumps from 100 to roughly 150 under the sand.
label: analysis

text: A Pokemon with the ability Magic Bounce reflects all status moves, including entry hazards, Taunt, and Thunder Wave, back at the user. This creates a hard mathematical counter to dedicated hazard setters like Ferrothorn and Skarmory.
label: analysis

text: The Choice Band multiplies the user's physical Attack by 1.5x but locks them into their first selected move. If you predict incorrectly and the opponent switches in a Ghost-type against your Choice Band locked Normal-type attack, you lose 100% of your momentum.
label: analysis

text: The ability Technician boosts the power of all moves with a base power of 60 or less by 50%. This elevates weak priority moves like Bullet Punch from a measly 40 power to a respectable 60 power before applying STAB, making Scizor a top-tier threat.
label: analysis

text: Leftovers restores exactly 1/16th of a Pokemon's maximum HP at the end of every turn. To maximize this recovery, competitive players specifically breed and EV train their Pokemon to have an HP stat that is a perfect multiple of 16 plus one.
label: analysis

text: Dragon Dance raises both Attack and Speed by one stage. Mathematically, a single Dragon Dance boosts both stats to 150% of their original value, allowing a bulky Dragonite to outspeed the entire unboosted metagame while hitting like a truck.
label: analysis

text: The move Earthquake has a base power of 100 and 100% accuracy, making it the most consistent physical spread move in the game. In double battles, its damage is reduced by 25% because it hits multiple targets, but it remains a staple on almost every physical attacker.
label: analysis

text: Mold Breaker ignores defensive abilities when attacking. This means an Excadrill with Mold Breaker can hit a Rotom-Wash with Earthquake, completely bypassing Rotom's Levitate ability and dealing super-effective damage.
label: analysis

text: The item Life Orb boosts the damage of all attacking moves by 30% but costs the user 10% of their maximum HP per attack. It provides incredible flexibility compared to Choice items, but the recoil mathematically limits a sweeper to a maximum of 10 attacks.
label: analysis

text: STAB combined with the Terastallize mechanic allows a Pokemon to double its type bonus. A Water-type Pokemon that Terastallizes into a Water-type will see its STAB multiplier jump from 1.5x to 2.0x, turning standard moves into nuke-level attacks.
label: analysis

text: The move Spore is the only sleep-inducing move in the game with 100% accuracy. However, Grass-type Pokemon, Pokemon with the Overcoat ability, and Pokemon holding Safety Goggles are entirely immune to powder-based moves, completely nullifying Spore.
label: analysis

text: Iron Barbs and Rough Skin both deal damage equal to 1/8th of the attacker's maximum HP whenever they make physical contact. Stacking this with a Rocky Helmet, which deals an additional 1/6th of max HP, punishes physical attackers for roughly 29% of their health per hit.
label: analysis
